title: Vivi Faces Her Pursuers
description: On pages 9–12, vivi faces her pursuers as vivi, Igaram, and the Straw Hats try to survive Baroque Works’ pursuit while the truth about Alabasta comes into focus.
scene: Vivi Faces Her Pursuers. Vivi, Igaram, and the Straw Hats try to survive Baroque Works’ pursuit while the truth about Alabasta comes into focus. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Igaram
dialogue.text: You honestly believe that we'd be ordered to come all the way out here at the edge of Grand Line,

dialogue.speaker: Miss Valentine
dialogue.text: Just to help you fight off a pesky swordsman? KyaHaha!

dialogue.speaker: Igaram
dialogue.text: Then what Busi- Mess have you come here for...?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Surely you must have some hunches...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: A crime severe enough that the boss would personally dispatch us all the way out here...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: The boss' direct words were, "My secret has been found out."

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Of course, we have no idea what that secret may be but...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No one is allowed to pry into the real identities of our members.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Thus, it's inexcusable for our boss' real identity to be known.

dialogue.speaker: Igaram
dialogue.text: It seems an agent of a certain kingdom has infiltrated into baroque works.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Wha...! W-wait! I know I wear a crown and all but I'm not really a king!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Valentine
dialogue.text: They've found about out every- thing...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Not good... Luffy's caught out in the open...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I won't let you harm her!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I swear it on my honour as the head of Alabasta's Royal Guards!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on vivi faces her pursuers. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
